title: Census the protocol for implied data items before defining forms
description: Before any form is defined, the protocol-analysis skill performs one complete pass over the protocol enumerating every data item the protocol implies must be captured anywhere in the build, regardless of which form will host it. The output is a protocol_data_item_census block (one row per item) capturing item_id, form_target, description, source_section, source_quote, type_hint, and choice_hint. This census is the master list against which §0.C reconciles the form-definition lookup hierarchy's output. Sources to scan: consent/eligibility/withdrawal/demographics sections, the Schedule of Assessments table, endpoint definitions, investigator obligations, glossary/definitions, document header metadata. Extraction signals include prose patterns 'subject must sign/date/confirm/report/undergo X', 'primary endpoint is X', 'randomization stratified by X', 'will be collected at each visit'.
rationale: The protocol is the authoritative source for which data items exist in a study. Without a census pass, the skill is reduced to inferring data items from incidental mentions in the form-definition library, which produces silent omissions (a data item the protocol called for that no library source covered). Silent omission is the worst failure mode for a study build — it surfaces months later in monitoring or data review when the missing item is needed for analysis. The census makes the protocol's full data-item set explicit and addressable in §0.C reconciliation.

history.note: Imported from conventions.md §0.A during Phase B.1d. Advisory because the rule documents the skill's upstream operation — protocol extraction happens before the conventions engine evaluates anything. The engine cannot 'apply' the census; it can only consume the spec that the census produced. Renders into Claude's prompt context during builds (per Phase A advisory-rendering design). First convention in the new `process.*` top-level category, which holds rules about how the build pipeline operates rather than rules about what it produces.
